hook.text: Lechuguilla Cave in New Mexico holds formations so rare that only scientists may enter.

buildup.text: Sulfuric acid from below, not surface water, carved its passages—a process called hypogene speleogenesis.

discovery.text: Its gypsum chandeliers, cave pearls, and hydromagnesite balloons exist almost nowhere else.

twist.text: Bacteria found in its walls may be millions of years old and resistant to modern antibiotics.

climax.text: Lechuguilla is both a geological wonder and a potential source of new medicines.

punchline.text: The cure for superbugs might be hiding in a cave.

quiz.question: What unusual process formed Lechuguilla Cave?
